texto: The dazzle of illusion, even when we see through it; the ways in which we entangle ourselves, the ways in which we work free-How do we get to change?When all is said and done, it seems a matter of grace.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de U.R. Ananthamurthy ಯು. ಆರ್. ಅನ೦ತಮೂರ್ತಿ que provoca temas ligados a trabalho. Ao questionar “The dazzle of illusion, even when we see through it; the ways in which we entangle ourselves, the…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre trabalho, grace, illusion, com foco em mudanca e trabalho.

frase_original: The dazzle of illusion, even when we see through it; the ways in which we entangle ourselves, the ways in which we work free-How do we get to change?When all is said and done, it seems a matter of grace.

texto: In a moment, at the very center of the swept and cleaned veranda, she had drawn two intersecting triangles, one upward- pointing and the other downward-pointing. In one, god's grace descended from heaven to earth; in the other, the soul ascended, aspiring toward god. Because of Sitamma's faultless eye, both met in perfect harmony.
explicacao: Citação de U.R. Ananthamurthy ಯು. ಆರ್. ಅನ೦ತಮೂರ್ತಿ sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. “In a moment, at the very center of the swept and cleaned veranda, she had drawn two intersecting …” articula tempo, god, harmony em registro de tempo, com relevância para contextos como termino e harmony.
